SEARCH
Package com.societegenerale.failover.store.multitenant

Interface TenantStoreFactory<T>

Type Parameters:
T - the type of the payload
Functional Interface:
This is a functional interface and can therefore be used as the assignment target for a lambda expression or method reference.
@FunctionalInterface public interface TenantStoreFactory<T>
SPI for creating a raw FailoverStore for a given tenant.

Threading contract

create(java.lang.String) is always called on the calling (request) thread, never inside an executor or any other thread. This is guaranteed by the fact that MultiTenantFailoverStore is always the outermost decorator in the chain: 

   Calling thread:
     MultiTenantFailoverStore.store(payload)
       → tenantStore()              ← reads TenantContext (ThreadLocal) here, on calling thread
       → factory.create(tenantId)   ← called here if first access for this tenant
       → FailoverStoreAsync.store() ← submits to executor; no ThreadLocal needed inside lambda
         → executor thread: DefaultFailoverStore.store() → rawStore.store()

Because create(java.lang.String) is called before any thread boundary is crossed, implementations may safely read ThreadLocal values (e.g. to select a schema or DataSource for the current tenant), but they must NOT store those values and rely on them being available later on a different thread.

In single-tenant mode create(java.lang.String) is called once at startup with SINGLE_TENANT_ID; implementations may ignore the tenant ID in that case.

Author:
Anand Manissery

    SINGLE_TENANT_ID

  • Field Details

    • SINGLE_TENANT_ID

      static final String SINGLE_TENANT_ID
      Sentinel tenant ID used in single-tenant mode. Passed to create(java.lang.String) once at application startup; implementations may ignore it.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Method Details

    • create

      FailoverStore<T> create(String tenantId)
      Creates a raw FailoverStore for the given tenantId.

      Always called on the calling (request) thread — never from inside an executor. See class-level Javadoc for the threading guarantee.

      Parameters:
      tenantId - the tenant identifier; SINGLE_TENANT_ID in single-tenant mode
      Returns:
      raw FailoverStore for that tenant (decorator wrapping is applied by the assembler)
